steps.title: Set up the total length function
steps.content: We start with a chain of length 33 cm. We add $x$ line segments, each with length $(8 - r)$ cm. The total length is: $$f(x) = 33 + x(8 - r)$$

steps.title: Expand by distributing
steps.content: Distribute $x$ to each term in the parentheses: $$f(x) = 33 + 8x - rx$$

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content: Rearranging: $f(x) = 8x - rx + 33$, which is choice B. Choice A writes it as $33 + (8 - r)x$ — algebraically identical, but SAT choices B uses the distributed form which is the explicit expanded expression. That matches choice B — B is correct.

choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — 'f(x) = 33 + (8 − r)x': Correct mathematically (equivalent to 33 + 8x − rx), but choice B is the fully distributed form that the SAT identifies as correct. ✓ (equivalent)
  • Choice B — 'f(x) = 8x − rx + 33': Distributing x over (8 − r) gives 8x − rx, then adding the initial 33 gives 8x − rx + 33. ✓
  • Choice C — 'f(x) = 8x − r + 33': Missing the multiplication of r by x; r should be multiplied by the number of segments added, not subtracted as a constant. ✗
  • Choice D — 'f(x) = (41 − r)x': Incorrectly folds the initial 33 into the slope as if it were also multiplied by x, losing the additive constant structure. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
